title: The Half-Life

calculator_tile.tagline: Watch the dollar’s purchasing power decay — half by half, generation by generation.
Corporate treasuries against monetary debasement — bitcoin as the dollar-melt antidote.

_comment: Moved from The Numbers to The Arguments (2026-06): a conceptual/philosophical page, not a data/tool page. Placed in 'Why Fiat Fails' next to The Melting Ice Cube as its direct mirror — fiat's share melts, your bitcoin share stays fixed. Page slug stays the-fixed-pie; slug, content, and OG card are unchanged — only the nav home moved. Tile name 'The Bitcoin Fixed Share' matches registry title; the tile uses no anchor (single-pane page).
title: The Bitcoin Fixed Share

calculator_tile.tagline: What does owning a piece of 21 million actually mean as a share of all the bitcoin there will ever be?

_comment: ── The Numbers — grouped via the `group` field; see SITE_GUIDE §30 (nav grouping). Group display order is controlled by base.njk's numbersGroups array; item order within a group follows array order here. ──
_comment2: Title in registry uses & entity (for HTML safety in nav); tile_title here overrides to 'Bitcoin & The Power Law' for the tile body (which we let Nunjucks render through autoescape-off via |safe).
title: Bitcoin & The Power Law

calculator_tile.tagline: Where does bitcoin sit inside the channel — floor, trend, upper band — that anchors every projection on this site?

_comment: Title uses & entity for nav HTML safety (rendered via | safe), matching the-power-law. Sibling to /the-power-law (price-vs-adoption to its price-vs-time). Interactive data explorer (proxy/era selector + fit chart); no calculator_tile — not a personal-decision tool, like the-doubling-ladder and heatmap.
title: Bitcoin & Metcalfe's Law

_comment: The Heatmap was previously on /calculators as proof-of-concept for live mini-renders, then moved to /the-gallery as a visual surface. Intentionally NOT on /calculators now (no calculator_tile block).
title: The Bitcoin Heatmap

calculator_tile.tagline: The wealth-over-time stress test — bitcoin against the S&P 500 and NASDAQ-100 across any holding window since 2010.
Bitcoin or a house — looking back, or projecting forward? Full mortgage and ownership costs included.
Bitcoin or rental property — the honest yield comparison once landlord costs and tax leakage are surfaced.
When can I retire on bitcoin? Drag the sliders — see how much you need, when, and on what income.
Turn the Power Law channel into a protocol — sell at upper-band triggers, rebuy at lower.
How much is the question — the Bell Labs sizing formula's startling answer, and the honest fraction of it.
Long-range Power Law projection across an investor’s lifetime — floor, trend, upper band.
Deploy a windfall now or ladder in — and how channel position changes the answer.
Lump, ladder, or hybrid — model your own deployment, looking back and looking forward.
When does borrowing make more sense than selling? Model liquidation distance vs. retained-coin upside.
Buy the house with bitcoin as collateral — the structural mortgage trade-off across counterparty tiers.

_comment: Living on Bitcoin is a personal-decision tool but currently does not have a /calculators tile. Add a calculator_tile block when wiring it in.
title: Living on Bitcoin

_comment: Display title is 'Bitcoin and Fixed Income' (renamed 2026-06 from 'Bitcoin Fixed Income' — the old name implied an inherent yield/fixed-income component bitcoin doesn't have). Slug stays /bitcoin-fixed-income (slug≠title is fine; renaming a live slug would need a 301). Editorial page H1 remains 'The Income Question'.
title: Bitcoin and Fixed Income

calculator_tile.tagline: Bitcoin-backed preferreds vs holding bitcoin — the bear-case insurance trade, with the actual crossover horizon and stress behavior shown honestly.

_comment: Hub-level page. The 'hub' category is intentionally outside the foundations/arguments/numbers nav buckets — base.njk only iterates those three. This entry exists so the related component can render Gallery back-link cards (its slug lookup against explorations.json otherwise silently fails). If a future nav iterator needs to include hub pages, add an explicit check for 'hub' there.
title: The Gallery
